title: Conditions satisfaction at filing dates
text: Unless otherwise provided in this Chapter, an issuer making an initial public offer of specified securities shall satisfy the conditions of this Chapter as on the date of filing of the draft offer document with the Board and also as on the date of 25[filing] the offer document with the Registrar of Companies.

source.span_hint: Unless otherwise provided in this Chapter

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (a) Debarment condition
text: if the issuer, any of its promoters, promoter group or directors or selling shareholders are debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: issuer any of its promoters promoter group or directors

chapter.title: MISCELLANEOUS

title: (b) Debarred Promoter/Director Condition
text: if any of the promoters or directors of the issuer is a promoter or director of any other company which is debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: if any of the promoters or directors

chapter.title: MISCELLANEOUS

title: (c) wilful defaulter or fraudulent borrower condition
text: if the issuer or any of its promoters or directors is a 26[wilful defaulter or a fraudulent borrower].

source.span_hint: issuer or any of its promoters or directors is a

chapter.title: MISCELLANEOUS

title: Debarred persons exemption
text: The restrictions under (a) and (b) above shall not apply to the persons or entities mentioned therein, who were debarred in the past by the Board and the period of debarment is already over as on the date of filing of the draft offer document with the Board.

source.span_hint: The restrictions under a and b above shall not apply

chapter.title: MISCELLANEOUS

title: Ineligibility for IPO due to outstanding convertible securities or rights
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if there are any outstanding convertible securities or any other right which would entitle any person with any option to receive equity shares of the issuer:

source.span_hint: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer

chapter.title: MISCELLANEOUS

title: (1)(a) Net Tangible Assets Requirement
text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets:

maps_to.constraints_text: at least three crore rupees, not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets

source.span_hint: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Net tangible asset threshold for monetary assets utilization
text: Provided that if more than fifty per cent. of the net tangible assets are held in monetary assets, the issuer has utilised or made firm commitments to utilise such excess monetary assets in its business or project;

source.span_hint: if more than fifty per cent of the net tangible assets

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Limit of fifty per cent on monetary assets exception for OFS
text: the limit of fifty per cent. on monetary assets shall not be applicable in case the initial public offer is made entirely through an offer for sale.

source.span_hint: the limit of fifty per cent on monetary assets

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (b) Average Operating Profit Requirement
text: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years;

source.span_hint: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (c) Net Worth Condition
text: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis;

source.span_hint: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Limit of fifty per cent on monetary assets exception
text: Provided further that the limit of fifty per cent. on monetary assets shall not be applicable in case the initial public offer is made entirely through an offer for sale.
notes: This clause provides an exception to a rule and does not map directly to a measurable issuer fact.

source.span_hint: limit of fifty per cent on monetary assets

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Revenue requirement if name changed recently
text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name within the last one year

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Eligibility for IPO through book-building process
text: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issue is made through the book-building process and the issuer undertakes to allot at least seventy five per cent. of the net offer to qualified institutional buyers and to refund the full subscription money if it fails to do so.

maps_to.constraints_text: at least seventy five per cent

source.span_hint: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Technology Intensive Requirement
text: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: the issuer shall be intensive

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Net worth threshold for SR shareholders
text: the net worth of the SR shareholder, as determined by a Registered Valuer, shall not be more than rupees one thousand crore.

source.span_hint: the net worth of the SR shareholder

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: SR shareholder net worth calculation
text: While determining the individual net worth of the SR shareholder, his investment/ shareholding in other listed companies shall be considered but not that of his shareholding in the issuer company.
notes: This clause specifies how to calculate the net worth of an SR shareholder without considering their shareholding in the issuer company, which cannot be directly mapped to a specific field.

source.span_hint: While determining the individual net worth

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (iii) SR shares issuance condition
text: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters/ founders who hold an executive position in the issuer company;
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as a stable fact about the issuer or offer.

source.span_hint: The SR shares were issued only to

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (iv) SR equity shares authorization
text: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized by a special resolution passed at a general meeting of the shareholders of the issuer, where the notice calling for such general meeting specifically provided for -

source.span_hint: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (1)(a) Application for listing approval
text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX;

source.span_hint: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (1)(b) Agreement with Depository
text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;

source.span_hint: it has entered into agreement with depository

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (c) Dematerialisation requirement for promoters' holdings
text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (d) partly paid-up equity shares condition
text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid up equity shares

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Finance arrangements for specific project
text: it has made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means towards seventy five per cent. of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from the issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the proposed public issue or through existing identifiable internal accruals.

source.span_hint: it has made firm arrangements of finance

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: General corporate purposes limit
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: The amount for general corporate purposes

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Track record consideration for spun-off division
text: In case of an issuer formed out of a division of an existing company, the track record of distributable profits of the division spun-off shall be considered only if the requirements regarding financial statements as provided for partnership firms or limited liability partnerships in Explanation (II) are complied with.

source.span_hint: In case of an issuer formed out of a division

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: adequate disclosures in financial statements
text: adequate disclosures are made in the financial statements as required to be made by the issuer as per schedule III of the Companies Act, 2013;

source.span_hint: adequate disclosures are made in the financial statements

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (II)(b)(i) - Accounts and Disclosures
text: the accounts and the disclosures made are in accordance with the provisions of schedule III of the Companies Act, 2013;

source.span_hint: the accounts and the disclosures made

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (Provided) Holding Period Consideration
text: in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.
notes: The holding period for convertible securities and resultant equity shares together must be considered to meet the one-year requirement.

source.span_hint: in case the equity shares received

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: holding_period_at_draft_filing
text: such holding period of one year shall be required to be complied with at the time of filing of the draft offer document.

source.span_hint: such holding period of one year

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (Explanation)
text: If the equity shares arising out of the conversion or exchange of the fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities are being offered for sale, the conversion or exchange should be completed prior to filing of the offer document (i.e. red herring prospectus in the case of a book built issue and prospectus in the case of a fixed price issue), provided full disclosures of the terms of conversion or exchange are made in the draft offer document.

source.span_hint: If the equity shares arising out of conversion or exchange

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Minimum holding period for shares offered to public
text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document:

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Limit on OFS by large shareholders
text: shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than fifty per cent of their pre-issue shareholding on fully diluted basis;

maps_to.constraints_text: not exceed more than fifty per cent

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale to the public

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Offer limit for small shareholders
text: shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, less than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than ten per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer on fully diluted basis;

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for major shareholders
text: for shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis, provisions of lock-in as specified under regulation 17 of these regulations shall be

source.span_hint: for shareholder(s) holding more than twenty per cent

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Default payment condition
text: Provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.

maps_to.constraints_text: for a period of more than six months

source.span_hint: Provided that it is not in default of payment

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: No default on debt payments
text: it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.

maps_to.constraints_text: for a period of more than six months

source.span_hint: it is not in default of payment

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: (1)(b) Debenture Trustee Appointment
text: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993;

source.span_hint: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: (c) Debenture Redemption Reserve
text: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder;
notes: This clause refers to compliance with external regulations (Companies Act) and does not provide a measurable check.

source.span_hint: it shall create debenture redemption reserve

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: (d) Charge or Security Condition
text: if the issuer proposes to create a charge or security on its assets in respect of secured convertible debt instruments, it shall ensure that:

source.span_hint: issuer proposes to create charge or security

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: (d)(iii) consent for second or pari passu charge
text: where security is already created on such assets in favour of any existing lender or security trustee or the issue of convertible debt instruments is proposed to be secured by creation of security on a leasehold land, the consent of such lender or security trustee or lessor for a second or pari passu charge has been obtained and submitted to the debenture trustee before the opening of the issue;

source.span_hint: security is already created on such assets

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Issuer Conditions for Initial Public Offer of Convertible Debt Instruments
text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions:
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check or constraint that can be represented as a stable issuer/offer fact.

source.span_hint: In addition to other requirements laid down

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Security or asset cover calculation for convertible debt instruments
text: the security or asset cover shall be arrived at after reduction of the liabilities having a first or prior charge, in case the convertible debt instruments are secured by a second or subsequent charge.
notes: This clause describes how to calculate security or asset cover for convertible debt instruments but does not provide a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: security or asset cover shall be arrived

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Issuer's obligation on converting optionally convertible debt
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Convertible Debt Instruments Option Clause
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares:

source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Redemption of unconverted convertible debt instruments
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.

source.span_hint: Where an option is to be given

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Exception to redemption disclosure requirement
text: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply if such redemption is as per the disclosures made in the offer document.
notes: This clause provides an exception to a disclosure requirement under another regulation, and does not impose a measurable check or condition that can be represented as a stable issuer/offer fact.

source.span_hint: The provision of sub-regulation (2)

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Convertible Debt Instruments Financing Restriction
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or for providing loans to or for acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies:

source.span_hint: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Convertible Debt Instruments Option Exemption
text: Provided that where the upper limit on the price of such convertible debt instruments and justification thereon is determined and disclosed to the investors at the time of making the issue, it shall not be necessary to give such option to the holders of the convertible debt instruments for converting the convertible portion into equity share capital within the said upper limit.

source.span_hint: Provided that where the upper limit on

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Eligibility for fully convertible debt instruments
text: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.

source.span_hint: issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Issuer eligibility for issuing warrants
text: An issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants in an initial public offer subject to the following:
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as a stable fact about the issuer or offer.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: 25% upfront consideration for formula-based warrants
text: Provided that in case the exercise price of warrants is based on a formula, twenty-five per cent. consideration amount based on the cap price of the price band determined for the linked equity shares or convertible securities shall be received upfront.

source.span_hint: in case the exercise price of warrants is based

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Promoter minimum holding requirement
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital:

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors contribution limit
text: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s).

maps_to.constraints_text: ten per cent. of the post-issue capital

notes: The rule specifies a maximum contribution limit for alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors, etc., without being identified as promoters.

source.span_hint: in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Exemption for Promoter Contribution Requirement
text: Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.span_hint: provided further that requirement minimum promoters contribution

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Warrant Tenure Limit
text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;

source.span_hint: tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Warrant Exercise Price Consideration
text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;

source.span_hint: the price or formula for determination

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Forfeiture of unexercised warrant consideration
text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by  the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: in case the warrant holder does not exercise

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

part.title: ISSUE OF CONVERTIBLE DEBT INSTRUMENTS AND WARRANTS

title: Promoter contribution requirement
text: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent. as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), as the case may be, either by way of equity shares38[, including SR equity shares held, if any,] or by way of subscription to convertible securities:

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter Contribution for Undisclosed Conversion Price
text: Provided that if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.

maps_to.constraints_text: shall contribute only by way of subscription

notes: The promoters must undertake to subscribe to equity shares upon conversion if the price is not pre-determined and disclosed.

source.span_hint: if the price of the equity shares allotted

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter Contribution Price
text: in case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: in case of any issue

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter contribution for convertible debt instruments
text: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares

maps_to.constraints_text: at least twenty per cent. of the project cost
contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares

source.span_hint: subject to the provisions of clause (a)

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoters' compliance deadline
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: The promoters shall satisfy

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter contribution escrow requirement
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds:

source.span_hint: In case the promoters have to subscribe

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Minimum promoter contribution for partly paid shares IPOs
text: Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.

maps_to.constraints_text: at least one hundred crore rupees

source.reg: Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares
source.span_hint: the minimum promoters contribution is more than

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (1)(a) - Specified Securities Acquired Conditions
text: specified securities acquired during the preceding three years, if these are: acquired for consideration other than cash and revaluation of assets or capitalisation of intangible assets is involved in such transaction; or resulting from a bonus issue by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealised profits of the issuer or from bonus issue against equity shares which are ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution;
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: specified securities acquired during the preceding three years

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (1)(b) - Price Comparison Requirement
text: specified securities acquired by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 40[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)], during the preceding one year at a price lower than the price at which specified securities are being offered to the public in the initial public offer:

maps_to.constraints_text: during the preceding one year at a price lower than the price at which specified securities are being offered to the public in the initial public offer

source.span_hint: specified securities acquired by the promoters

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (a) Promoter's Contribution Basis
text: Promoters’ contribution shall be computed on the basis of the post-issue expanded capital: assuming full proposed conversion of convertible securities into equity shares;

source.span_hint: Promoters contribution shall be computed

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (b) Outstanding Employee Stock Options
text: assuming exercise of all vested options, where any employee stock options or stock appreciation rights are outstanding at the time of initial public offer in terms of proviso (a) to sub-regulation (2) of regulation 5.
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer or offer fact. It is context for understanding the conditions under which employee stock options and stock appreciation rights are considered outstanding at the time of IPO.

source.span_hint: assuming exercise of all vested options

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (a)(i) non-cash consideration
text: acquired for consideration other than cash and revaluation of assets or capitalisation of intangible assets is involved in such transaction;
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: acquired for consideration other than cash

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (a)(ii) - Bonus Issue Contribution Exclusion
text: resulting from a bonus issue by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealised profits of the issuer or from bonus issue against equity shares which are ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution;
notes: This clause provides an exclusion to the requirement of minimum promoter's contribution in certain cases involving bonus issues. It does not impose a measurable check that can be represented as a stable issuer/offer fact.

source.span_hint: resulting from a bonus issue

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Exemption for certain entities paying difference in IPO price
text: nothing contained in this clause shall apply: if the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 42[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)], as applicable, pay to the issuer the difference between the price at which the specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and the price at which the specified securities had been acquired;

maps_to.constraints_text: holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital

notes: This rule provides an exemption if certain entities pay the difference between IPO price and acquisition price.

source.span_hint: nothing contained in this clause shall apply

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (ii) Investment Period Condition
text: if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government, as applicable, by the promoters in lieu of business and invested capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval;
notes: The condition specifies that the invested capital must have existed for more than one year prior to approval.

source.span_hint: if such specified securities are acquired

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Initial public offer for government companies and special purpose vehicles
text: to an initial public offer by a government company, statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up by any of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector;
notes: This clause does not specify a measurable check that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: initial public offer by a government company

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter holding period for compulsorily convertible securities
text: to equity shares arising from the conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities, including depository receipts, that have been held by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document and such fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities are converted or exchanged into equity shares prior to the filing of the offer document (i.e., red herring prospectus in case of a book built issue

source.span_hint: promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter lock-in period
text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable (hereinafter referred to as “lock-in”) for the periods as stipulated hereunder:

source.span_hint: The specified securities held promoters

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Promoter lock-in period
text: promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: promoters holding in excess

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for capital expenditure use
text: in case the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be one year from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: in case the majority of the issue proceeds

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Definition of Capital Expenditure
text: For the purpose of this sub-regulation, “capital expenditure” shall include civil work, miscellaneous fixed assets, purchase of land, building and plant and machinery, etc. and repayment of existing loan(s) that may have been taken for the purpose of such capital expenditure.
notes: This clause provides a definition rather than a measurable check or condition.

source.span_hint: For the purpose of this sub-regulation

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in until conversion into equity shares having voting rights same as that of ordinary shares or shall be locked-in for a period specified in sub-regulations (1), whichever is later.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for VCF/AIF purchases
text: such equity shares shall be locked in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase by the venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of Category I or Category II or foreign venture capital investor.

source.span_hint: such equity shares shall be locked in

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for non-promoter pre-issue capital
text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer:

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital held

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Employee equity shares disclosure requirement
text: equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme of the issuer prior to the initial public offer, if the issuer has made full disclosures with respect to such options or scheme in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI;

source.span_hint: equity shares allotted employees

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: (c) equity shares held by funds/investors
text: equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor:
notes: This clause does not specify any measurable check that can be represented as an issuer/offer fact.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture capital fund

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in provisions exemption for green shoe option
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of lending of such specified securities and ending on the date on which they are returned to the lender in terms of sub-regulation (5) or (6) of regulation 57
notes: This clause provides an exemption from lock-in provisions for specified securities lent to a stabilising agent for green shoe option purposes, but does not specify any measurable check that can be represented as a stable issuer/offer fact.

source.span_hint: The lock-in provisions shall not apply

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for returned securities
text: Provided that the specified securities shall be locked-in for the remaining period from the date on which they are returned to the lender

source.span_hint: the specified securities shall be locked-in

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for partly paid-up securities
text: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in are partly paid-up and the amount called-up on such specified securities is less than the amount called-up on the specified securities issued to the public, the lock-in shall end only on the expiry of three years after such specified securities have become pari passu with the specified securities issued to the public.

source.span_hint: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in inscription for dematerialized securities
text: The certificates of specified securities which are subject to lock-in shall contain the inscription “non-transferable” and specify the lock-in period and in case such specified securities are dematerialised, the issuer shall ensure that the lock-in is recorded by the depository.

source.span_hint: The certificates of specified securities which are subject

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Pledging of locked-in securities by promoters
text: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following:
notes: This clause does not specify any measurable constraint that can be represented as an issuer fact.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period for pledged securities
text: Provided that such lock-in shall continue pursuant to the invocation of the pledge and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer the specified securities till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: such lock-in shall continue

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Transfer of locked-in securities
text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred:
notes: This clause does not impose a measurable check but rather outlines conditions under which certain transfers can occur.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD

title: Lock-in period continuation for specified securities
text: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: lock-in on such specified securities shall continue

chapter.title: INITIAL PUBLIC OFFER ON MAIN BOARD
